My Report
Regex · ^start · end$ · a|b · . any · .* seq · \d digit · [abc] set
  • 1 Grid of cells
  • 2 Matplotlib and code cells
  • 3 Iframes, Plotly and Tables

Grid of cells

Example of grid and basic cell types

Original Cylinder without strakes

Cylinder with strake a

Cylinder with strake b

You can build a customizable grid using various cell types, featuring:

  • Flexible Layouts: Merge multiple cells together to create larger display areas.
  • Smart Positioning: Place cells by explicitly setting their row and column coordinates (using 1-based indexing), or let the system automatically find the next available slot.
  • Rich Text Support: Populate text cells with Markdown, including inline LaTeX for math equations like $f_n=\sqrt{k/m}$.
  • Dynamic Metrics: Display key performance indicators.
  • Figures: Embed images. Click on the images to view them in full size.
0.75
Max Displacement / Diameter
0.25
Max Displacement / Diameter
▼ -66 % Compared to original cylinder
0.125
Max Displacement / Diameter
▼ -75 % Compared to original cylinder

Reference: Dessimoni, A. (2021). Modelagem e aplicação de métodos de fronteira imersa para análise de escoamentos sobre atenuadores de VIV (Master's thesis, Universidade Federal de Uberlândia).

Matplotlib and code cells

Looping is a good way to automate the creation of multiple similar cells. The figures were created using the code snippet below (which used the add_code method).

Note that matplotlib figure objects can be directly added to the slide using the add_matplotlib method.

for f in range(4):
    i, j = (f//2 + 1), (f%2  + 1)
    x, y = sinoidal_example(f)
    
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    ax.plot(x, y)
    
    # --- Add matplotlib fig to cell
 
    slide.add_matplotlib(
        fig, fmt="png", 
        caption=f"$f={f}Hz$", 
        row=i, col=j
    )

$f=0Hz$

$f=1Hz$

$f=2Hz$

$f=3Hz$

Iframes, Plotly and Tables

Example of an iframe cell embedding a interactive map.

Example of a Plotly cell displaying an interactive scatter plot.

The iframe and Plotly cells are interactive, allowing you to embed live content and interactive visualizations directly into your deck.

You can also add a table cell to display a DataFrame, as shown below.

All cells support options to avoid overflow and add an expand button to view the full content.

country continent year lifeExp pop gdpPercap iso_alpha iso_num
